# GitHub Repository Settings Guide

After pushing to GitHub, configure these settings in the GitHub web UI for maximum discoverability:

## 1. Repository Description (Settings → General)

Set the description to:

```
🧠 Voice-first AI desktop agent — control your OS, automate workflows, search files semantically, and remote-control your Android phone. Local-first, open-source, built on Electron + React + Gemini.
```

This description is indexed by GitHub search and shown in Google results.

## 2. Repository Topics (Settings → General → Topics)

Add these topics (in this exact order — GitHub weights the first ones higher):

```
ai-desktop-assistant
voice-control
electron
desktop-automation
ai-agent
local-first
semantic-search
android-remote-control
workflow-automation
multimodal-ai
gemini
rag
typescript
react
open-source
adb
tesseract-ocr
vector-database
```

## 3. Social Preview Image (Settings → General → Social Preview)

Upload `assets/social_preview.png` (1280×640). This is the image shown when sharing the repo link on Twitter, Reddit, Discord, LinkedIn, etc.

## 4. Website URL (Settings → General)

Set to: `https://eliaiw.vercel.app`

## 5. Enable Discussions (Settings → General → Features)

Check "Discussions". This enables the community tab and increases engagement metrics.

## 6. Enable Sponsorship (Settings → Sponsor button)

The `FUNDING.yml` is already configured. Verify it appears on the repo.

## 7. Enable GitHub Pages (Settings → Pages)

Optional but powerful: Serve the `assets/` directory or create a `docs/` branch for SEO backlinks.

## 8. Pin Repository

Go to your GitHub profile → "Customize your pins" → Pin this repository. Pinned repos get 3-5x more views.
